'''''UFC 299: O'Malley vs. Vera 2''''' was a mixed martial arts event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on March 9, 2024, at the [[Kaseya Center]] in [[Miami, Florida]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/15/24003602/ufc-confirms-event-dates-locations-including-ufc-299-ufc-300|title=UFC confirms event dates, locations including UFC 299, UFC 300|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2023-12-15|access-date=2023-12-16}}</ref><ref name="Miami">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-299-set-miami-sean-omalley-vs-marlon-vera-rematch-title-headliner-march-9|title=UFC 299 set for Miami with Sean O'Malley vs. Marlon Vera 2 title headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2023-12-15|access-date=2023-12-17}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's third visit to Miami and first since [[UFC 287]] in April 2023.<ref name="Miami" />

A [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Sean O'Malley (fighter)|Sean O'Malley]] and [[Marlon Vera]] headlined the event.<ref name="Main">{{cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/.amp/news/sean-omalley-marlon-vera-headline-march-ufc-ppv|title=Sean O'Malley vs. Marlon Vera 2 Set To Headline UFC 299 Pay-Per-View In March|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Curtis Calhoun|date=2023-11-06|access-date=2023-11-06}}</ref> The pair previously met at [[UFC 252]] in August 2020 which Vera won by first round TKO.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-252-results-highlights-marlon-vera-stuns-injured-sean-omalley-with-vicious-tko-stoppage/|title=UFC 252 results, highlights: Marlon Vera stuns injured Sean O'Malley with vicious TKO stoppage|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Brian Campbell|date=2020-08-16|access-date=2023-11-06}}</ref> [[Merab Dvalishvili]] served as backup and potential replacement for this fight.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/3/5/24091937/merab-dvalishvili-set-to-serve-as-backup-to-ufc-299-main-event|title=Merab Dvalishvili set to serve as backup to UFC 299 main event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-03-05|access-date=2024-03-05}}</ref>

A five round lightweight bout between former interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Dustin Poirier]] and [[Benoît Saint Denis]] took place in the co-main event.<ref name="Comain">{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/8/24029566/dustin-poirier-vs-benoit-saint-denis-set-for-five-round-co-main-event-at-ufc-299|title=Dustin Poirier vs. Benoit Saint Denis set for five-round co-main event at UFC 299|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-01-07|access-date=2024-01-07}}</ref> The bout was briefly in jeopardy when Poirier indicated the bout would not take place as he had never signed a contract and he was unable to come to terms with the promotion.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-299-dustin-poirier-vs-benoit-saint-denis-canceled|title=Dustin Poirier says UFC 299 fight vs. Benoit Saint-Denis is off: 'We couldn't come to terms'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2024-02-01|access-date=2024-02-01}}</ref> However, Poirier shortly thereafter retracted his statement describing it as a misunderstanding.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/2/1/24058561/dustin-poirier-apologizes-misunderstanding-declares-fight-with-benoit-saint-denis-back-on-ufc-299|title=Dustin Poirier apologizes for 'misunderstanding,' declares fight with Benoit Saint Denis back on for UFC 299|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-02-01|access-date=2024-02-01}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Curtis Blaydes]] and [[Jailton Almeida]] took place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/12/23998755/jailton-almeida-vs-curtis-blaydes-rebooked-for-ufc-299-in-march|title=Jailton Almeida vs. Curtis Blaydes rebooked for UFC 299 in March|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2023-12-12|access-date=2023-12-12}}</ref> They were previously expected to headline [[UFC Fight Night: Almeida vs. Lewis]] in November 2023 but Blaydes withdrew for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/10/ufc-news-derrick-lewis-vs-jailton-almeida-sao-paulo-curtis-blaydes-out-mma-fight-booking-matchup|title=Derrick Lewis replaces Curtis Blaydes, headlines UFC Sao Paulo vs. Jailton Almeida|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Mike Bohn|date=2023-10-09|access-date=2023-12-12}}</ref>

[[Ian Machado Garry]] and [[Geoff Neal]] were expected to meet in a welterweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-299-news-ian-machado-garry-vs-geoff-neal-miami-pay-per-view-welterweight-grudge-match|title=After UFC 296 withdrawal, Ian Machado Garry gets booking against Geoff Neal at UFC 299|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2023-12-17|access-date=2023-12-17}}</ref> They were originally booked for [[UFC 292]] in August 2023, but Neal withdrew due to undisclosed health reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/08/ufc-292-geoff-neal-out-of-ufc-292-fight-vs-ian-machado-garry-boston-health-issues|title=Geoff Neal out of UFC 292 fight vs. Ian Machado Garry in Boston|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-08-09|access-date=2023-08-09}}</ref> However the bout was moved to [[UFC 298]] for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ian-garry-vs-geoff-neal-shifted-from-miami-to-ufc-298-in-anaheim|title=Ian Garry vs. Geoff Neal shifted from Miami to UFC 298 in Anaheim|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-12-21|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref>

Former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Bantamweight Championship|Invicta FC Bantamweight Champion]] (also former [[UFC Women's Flyweight Championship]] challenger) [[Lauren Murphy]] was expected to face [[Karine Silva]] in a women's flyweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/12/23998975/mateusz-gamrot-vs-rafael-dos-anjos-among-new-fights-added-to-ufc-299|title=Mateusz Gamrot vs. Rafael dos Anjos among new fights added to UFC 299|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2023-12-12|access-date=2024-01-15}}</ref> However, Murphy pulled out due to injury and Silva is now expected to face former [[Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki#Women's flyweight championship|KSW Women's Flyweight Champion]] [[Ariane Lipski]] at [[UFC on ESPN 55]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://agfight.com/noticias/karine-killer-fica-sem-adversaria-para-o-ufc-299-apos-lesao-de-lauren-murphy/|title=Karine Killer is left without an opponent for UFC 299 after injury to Lauren Murphy|publisher=agfight.com|author=Neri Fung|date=2024-01-12|access-date=2024-01-15}} {{in lang|pt-br}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com.br/mma/ufc/artigo/_/id/13100224/ariane-lipski-encara-karine-killer-no-ufc-em-abril|title=Ariane Lipski faces Karine Killer at UFC in April|publisher=espn.com.br|author=Ag. Fight|date=2024-01-15|access-date=2024-01-15}} {{in lang|pt-br}}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between [[Ion Cuțelaba]] and [[2018 in Professional Fighters League#2018 PFL Heavyweight playoffs|2018 PFL heavyweight tournament winner]] [[Philipe Lins]] took place at the event.<ref name="Rebook">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/2/9/24067891/philipe-lins-takes-on-ion-cutelaba-at-ufc-299|title=Philipe Lins takes on Ion Cutelaba at UFC 299|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2024-02-09|access-date=2024-02-09}}</ref> They were previously expected to meet at [[UFC Fight Night: Dawson vs. Green]] in October 2023 but Lins withdrew the day of the event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2023/10/7/23907740/report-ufc-vegas-80-loses-light-heavyweight-barnburner-in-final-moments-canceled-lins-cutelaba|title=Report: UFC Vegas 80 loses light heavyweight barn burner in final moments|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2023-10-06|access-date=2024-02-09}}</ref>

At the weigh ins, [[C.J. Vergara]] weighed in at 127 pounds, one pound over the flyweight non-title fight limit. His bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 30 percent of his purse which went to his opponent [[Asu Almabayev]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/03/ufc-299-weigh-ins-results-live-stream-omalley-vs-vera|title=UFC 299 weigh-in results, video: Fighter misses mark for third time|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Ken Hathaway|date=2024-03-08|access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship|UFC Women's Strawweight Champion]] [[Joanna Jędrzejczyk]] was announced as the next "modern wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during June's International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/03/ufc-news-joanna-jedrzejczyk-joins-2024-hall-of-fame-class|title=Former champ Joanna Jedrzejczyk joins 2024 UFC Hall of Fame class|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-03-09|access-date=2024-03-10}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/3/10/24095639/ufc-299-post-fight-bonus-winners-results-miami-ppv-espn-o-malley-blaydes-poirier-pereira-maddalena|title=Bonuses! Dustin Poirier's epic war with Benoit Saint Denis earns FOTN honors - UFC 299|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2024-03-09|access-date=2024-03-09}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Dustin Poirier vs. Benoît Saint Denis'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Sean O’Malley, Jack Della Maddalena, Curtis Blaydes, Michel Pereira''' and '''Robelis Despaigne'''
